heading: CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT OF NATURAL RESOURCES; DEVELOPMENT OF PARKS AND RECREATIONAL FACILITIES; CONSERVATION AND RECLAMATION DISTRICTS; INDEBTEDNESS AND TAXATION AUTHORIZED
operative_text: The conservation and development of all of the natural resources of this State, and development of parks and recreational facilities, including the control, storing, preservation and distribution of its storm and flood waters, the waters of its rivers and streams, for irrigation, power and all other useful purposes, the reclamation and irrigation of its arid, semi-arid and other lands needing irrigation, the reclamation and drainage of its overflowed lands, and other lands needing drainage, the conservation and development of its forests, water and hydro-electric power, the navigation of its inland and coastal waters, and the preservation and conservation of all such natural resources of the State are each and all hereby declared public rights and duties; and the Legislature shall pass all such laws as may be appropriate thereto.

defined_terms.definition_excerpt: which districts shall be governmental agencies and bodies politic and corporate with such powers of government and with the authority to exercise such rights, privileges and functions concerning the subject matter of this amendment as may be conferred by law

_note: Full section includes subsections (a) through (f) plus (c-1). Operative text captures core declaration in subsection (a). Subsection (c) requires voter approval before bond issuance. Subsection (c-1) expands authority for parks/recreational facilities financing in specified counties. Subsection (d) requires 30-90 day published notice and Texas Water Commission review before district-creating legislation. Subsection (e) requires notice to commissioners courts and municipal governing bodies. Subsection (f) authorizes fire-fighting activities.
